steps.title: Identify the subject's number
steps.content: ”A cycle of lunar phases” has “cycle” as its head noun, making the subject singular.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: ”Takes” is the only third-person singular present-tense form. “Are taking,” “have taken,” and “take” violate subject-verb agreement by providing plural or non-singular forms. That matches choice D — D is correct.
